primary_accounting_issue: Éligibilité et modèle de comptabilité de couverture applicable, dans les comptes consolidés, au risque de change lié à des dividendes intragroupe déjà comptabilisés en créance

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping hedge accounting eligibility and hedged-item guidance, so IFRS 9 governs and IAS 39 is discarded.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low: IFRS 9 directly contains the current hedge accounting requirements for hedged items in consolidated financial statements, including intragroup foreign currency exceptions.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern what can be a hedged item and when intragroup foreign currency exposures can be designated in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: This Interpretation clarifies the distinct hedge accounting model for a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation.
This Standard helps distinguish the reporting-level alternative of separate financial statements from consolidated financial statements for intragroup positions.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address presentation and classification of financial instruments and dividends, not hedge-accounting eligibility for intragroup FX risk.
These paragraphs deal with statement of cash flows classification, not the hedge-accounting models relevant to the question.
These paragraphs are disclosure-relief provisions and dividend disclosures, not relevant to identifying hedge accounting approaches here.
This Interpretation concerns non-cash distributions to owners, not intragroup dividend receivables as hedged items.
This Interpretation addresses classification of members' shares and related distributions, not hedge accounting.
This Interpretation concerns uncertain tax treatments and is unrelated to hedge accounting for intragroup dividends.
These paragraphs address EPS effects of preference dividends, not hedge accounting.
This Interpretation concerns service concession arrangements and is unrelated to the issue.
This Interpretation addresses extinguishing liabilities with equity instruments, not hedge accounting.
This Interpretation concerns levies and is unrelated to the issue.

treatment_families.family: IFRS hedge accounting models available for foreign currency exposures on recognised items or forecast transactions
Net investment hedge model for foreign operations
Reporting-level distinction between consolidated and separate financial statements for intragroup items

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes fair value hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities and foreign currency risk may be designated.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes cash flow hedge accounting as a top-level model, including for highly probable forecast transactions and certain qualifying intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 treat a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposure.
